description: Altera Capital Group ISSUER-SIDE divergence package (eighth test case). Deliberately small: Treasury, Compliance, Risk, and Marketing agents come UNCHANGED from the shared ecarmf.ai-capital-markets package (the same package instance Asset Fractional runs - the Agent Library reuse premise, proven). This package adds only what genuinely diverges: issuance-program KPIs with concentration and wallet-security risk tags, distribution-channel tracking across broker-dealer/ATS platforms (tZERO et al.), and the Token Operations agent. Executive Advisory rides the kernel's built-in Executive Advisor - not rebuilt. Ongoing investor compliance ('AccreditationRecheck') layers ComplianceRenewal on top of the InvestorProfile gate - Refinements 1 + 10 combined, configuration not code.
name: AI Altera Issuer

entities.description: One tokenized offering's issuance-program snapshot: raise progress, tokens issued, channel mix, concentration, wallet incidents.

capabilities.name: Token Issuance Operations
capabilities.description: Issuer-side raise, distribution-channel, concentration, and wallet-security intelligence.

schemaTemplates.name: Issuance program period snapshot (platform TBD - open question 2)

performanceFrameworks.name: Token Issuance Operations KPI Framework v1
performanceFrameworks.description: Issuer-side program KPIs. The risk list (Requirement 6) rides riskType tags on these scores - TreasuryConcentration and WalletSecurity here, joining Regulatory/Cybersecurity/Marketing/Reputational proven at prior tenants; a near-superset achieved with ZERO new risk entities.

agents.name: Token Operations Guide
agents.description: Issuer-side operations advisor: issuance progress, distribution-channel mix, settlement hygiene, concentration and wallet-security posture.
agents.persona: You are the token operations advisor for a digital securities issuer distributing directly and through regulated broker-dealer/ATS channels. You read issuance-program KPIs (raise progress, channel mix, investor concentration, wallet incidents) and risk-tagged deviations. You advise on distribution balance, settlement operations discipline, and concentration limits - always as recommendations; issuance decisions, channel agreements, and transfers are made by authorized officers. You never state token prices or terms - the offering documents govern.
sampleQuestions: How is the current raise progressing against target?
Is our distribution too dependent on one channel?
Are there concentration or wallet-security flags open?
agents.outputDisclaimer: Operational analytics only - not an offer, price indication, or securities advice. Offering terms are governed exclusively by the offering documents; distribution decisions rest with authorized officers and counsel.
